epigraph.text: Man can do what he wills, but he cannot will what he wills.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Texas HOA Bans Shadows, Fines Residents For 'Trespassing Light'
follyOfTheDay.summary: A suburban homeowners association in the Dallas-Fort Worth area has voted to levy fines against any resident whose shadow falls upon a neighbor's property during daylight hours. The ordinance, passed 4-3 by the HOA board, defines shadows as 'visual intrusions' and 'unauthorized light manipulation.' Residents now face fines of up to $500 per shadow incident, measured between 6 a.m. and 6 p.m. daily. The HOA president insisted the measure was necessary to maintain 'property integrity and solar autonomy,' while a spokesperson explained that shadows represent a 'cumulative erosion of visual boundaries.' Legal experts suggest the ordinance is likely unenforceable, though residents report the HOA is already collecting evidence via dated photographs.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: At last: the HOA has discovered the one thing more oppressive than actual regulations—the regulation of light itself. Hegel would weep.
